patch.value: Unknown-option errors give no 'did you mean' suggestion; --body (valid on create) is rejected on comments/notes/learnings with only generic advice

Repro: PM_AUTHOR=x pm comments pm-xxxx --body 'my update'. Observed (exit=2): 'Error: Unknown option --body ... What is required: Use supported options only, or move option to the correct subcommand. Examples: pm comments --help'. No suggestion of the actual correct flag (--add, or the positional [text] form). This is an easy agent trap because --body IS a real, canonical flag on pm create (-b, --body, shown in contracts), so an agent reasonably transfers it to comments/notes/learnings and gets stuck. Expected: when an unknown --flag is a near-miss of (or alias-equivalent to) a supported flag on this command, emit 'did you mean --add?'. Bootstrap already does silent typo-correction for some cases (e.g. --titl -> --title, --Title -> --title both accepted), but it does NOT fire for --body->--add (different word) or for transpositions (--titel rejected) / abbreviations (--desc rejected), and when it can't auto-fix it gives no suggestion. Severity: low-medium (agent friction; recoverable via --help but costs a round-trip). Scope note: existing did-you-mean items are for unknown IDs (pm-99x5), install aliases (pm-uuee), and plan subcommands (pm-zpa5) -- none cover unknown OPTION flags.

value.author: dogfood-agent
value.text: Dedup checks run against real tracker (no PM_PATH override) on 2026-05-25 before filing: (1) pm search 'did you mean unknown option flag suggestion near miss' --limit 12 -> pm-99x5(closed: did-you-mean for unknown IDs), pm-zpa5(closed: did-you-mean for pm list --type Plan), pm-uuee(closed: did-you-mean for install aliases), pm-ixi1(closed: stop listing provided --flag as missing), pm-bzyr(closed: required-option error guidance) -- NONE cover near-miss suggestions for unknown OPTION flags. (2) pm search 'comments body flag inconsistent add' --limit 10 -> pm-cvwi(closed: comments shorthand compatibility/docs parity) and pm-9y8q(closed: dedupe comments/notes/learnings stacks) -- these establish the positional [text]/--add shorthand but do not add a did-you-mean for --body or cross-command flag confusion. (3) pm list --status open --limit 30 -> pm-59gj,pm-gt82,pm-3cbk (unrelated). (4) pm list --status in-progress -> pm-rnpb,pm-7rlp,pm-cf1u (unrelated). Conclusion: no open or recently-closed item covers did-you-mean for unknown option flags. Filed new under pm-rnpb.

value.author: codex-full-audit
value.text: Partial fix from telemetry/manual audit: unknown-command recovery now includes common real-world aliases from telemetry (show -> get, comment -> comments, note -> notes, learning -> learnings), and append-style commands now suggest semantic replacements for common wrong flags (--body/--text/--comment -> --add). Verified with focused help-runtime tests and manual temp-project probes. Item should remain open for broader cross-command option guidance on search/list/config and deeper schema simplification.

value.author: manual-dogfood-specialist
value.text: Manual dogfood evidence 2026-05-25: command typo recovery for `pm commments ...` is now good and suggests `comments`. Remaining recovery friction found in fresh temp project: `pm test-all --json --type Task` fails with unknown_option and no did-you-mean/alternative, although many list/search commands accept --type; `pm guide search --json` correctly says available topics but suggested_retry becomes `pm guide search --json --list "<value>"`, which appears malformed versus the message saying `pm guide --list`. This fits the existing recovery-quality issue; no duplicate item created.

value.author: manual-dogfood-specialist
value.text: Additional manual dogfood recovery finding 2026-05-25: `pm list-all --sort updated --order desc` fails with allowed values `priority|deadline|updated_at|created_at|title|parent`, but the JSON error examples include `pm list-all --json --fields priority --sort updated --order desc`, repeating the invalid value. This is another recovery/example correctness issue for agents; use `updated_at` in docs/examples or accept `updated` as an alias.

value.author: codex-full-audit
value.text: Follow-up fix 2026-05-25: schema shorthand now accepts pm schema <TypeName> when add-type options make intent clear, reducing agent command-shape friction discovered by manual walkthrough. Files root contract drift also fixed: pm contracts --command files --flags-only no longer advertises discover-only --note/--apply, preventing agents from issuing rejected pm files ... --note commands.

value.author: manual-dogfood-subagent
value.text: Manual dogfood confirmed command-recovery pain points: a wrong assumed id prefix caused many item_not_found errors; recovery now includes list-open/search hints and active PM_PATH confirmation, which is useful. Unknown option pm update --add-tags failed with only update --help guidance; agent expected additive tag semantics from create/tag workflows. Config key output.default failed with a long supported-key list; useful but still schema-heavy for agents looking for output default customization.
